وقد فاز ابو بكر الصديق بالقسط الوافر من بركة اسم الله الوهاب، لأنه بذل جميع ماله في سبيل ربه حتى قال عليه الصلاة والسلام «وماذا ابقيت لأهلك يا ابا بكر؟ فأجاب: ابقيت لهم الله ورسوله» وان من يبذل جميع ما يملكه حتى الروح لوجه الله تعالى لا للوصول إلى نعيم الجنة او الحذر من عذاب النار فهو جدير بان يسمى عبدا وهابا، اللهم اجعلنا منهم يا كريم.
فاذكر مولاك : فإنه يرعاك في دنياك وأخراك، واسأل الوهاب من فضله، ولا ترج غيره، ولا تتوقع الخير إلا منه، فمن ذكر الوهاب فتح الله له كل باب .

ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف أنه من دوام عليه في السجود بعد صلاة الضحى يسر الله له الغنى، ومن قال «ربنا لا تزغ قلوبنا بعد إذ هديتنا وهب لنا من لدنك رحمة إنك أنت الوهاب» سبع مرات دبر كل صلاة حفظ من مكر الشيطان بإذنه تعالى.
فيا إلهي هب لنا عافية في ابداننا، وسعة في ارزاقنا وطول في اعمارنا، وهب لنا لذة المعرفة في قلوبنا والشهود لأرواحنا حتى نبذل النفس والمال بدون عوض ولا غرض إلى وجهك الكريم.
descriptionEnglish: The Bestower is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One Who bestows gifts for no return, and grants wishes without being requested.

• He is continually bestowing gifts and providing all needs, for no prompt or delayed return. So we do not imagine pure gifts and generosity coming except from Allah, Glory be to Him. Every favour we enjoy is a reflection of His generosity. There is no true bestower except Allah, and no true withholder except Allah. All acts of grace and favour are His.

Allah’s name, the Bestower, occurs a number of times in the Holy Quran -in Sūrah Āl Imrān: “Oh Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us. And bestow upon us a grace from You. Truly You are the Bestower.”⁽¹⁾ Also, the Almighty says “my Lord has bestowed good judgement upon me, and made me one of the Messengers.”⁽²⁾

In order to assume the name of Allah, the Bestower, one must be exceedingly shy of Allah, the Exalted. One must ask for own needs only from Allah; and one must entrust all one’s affairs to Allah. One must have a bestowing nature towards the servants of Allah in what they need from him. Meanwhile, one’s heart should be grateful for the blessings Allah bestowed, so as to deserve and win the pleasure of the Bestower.

Abū Bakr as-Siddique has greatly assumed the name of Allah, the Bestower. He spent all his wealth in the Path of Allah; when the Prophet asked “And what have you left for your family, Abū Bakr?” he replied: “I left them Allah and His Messenger.”⁽³⁾ Whoever spends all that he possesses- even his soul- for the sake of Allah, the Exalted- and not because he desires the blessings of Paradise or wants to guard against the Punishment of hell- such a one is worthy of being named a “bestowing slave”. Oh Allah, make us of them, oh the Generous.

Remember your Lord; He takes care of you in this world and the next. Ask the Bestower out of His grace. Never expect any favours from anyone else. Do not expect goodness except from Him. Allah will open all opportunities for whoever remembers Al-Wahhab.

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever continually recites during prostration in ṣalāh, Allah will facilitate wealth for him. And whoever says seven times after ṣalāh “Oh Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us. And bestow Your favour upon us. Truly You are the Bestower” – such a one will be protected from the machinations of the devil by the grace of Allah, the Exalted.

Oh God! Bestow health upon our bodies, extensive provisions and long lives. Bestow upon our hearts the taste of divine knowledge and upon our souls the witnessing of divine realities so that we expend our self and possessions for no return except for the sake of Your Noble Countenance.
refEnglish: (3)Abū Dāwūd, Tirmithi and Ḥākim relating ‘Umar bin al-Khaṭṭāb

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «وفي السماء رزقكم وما توعدون»
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى خالق الارزاق المتفضل بإيصالها إلى خلقه، فهو الذي يمد بفضله كل كائن بما يحفظ مادته وصورته فيمد العقول بالعلوم والارواح بالتجليات والابدان بالأغذية،
• وهو الذي يرزق الاشباح فوائد لطفه والارواح عوائد كشفه كما قال تعالى «الله لطيف بعباده يرزق من يشاء وهو القوي العزيز».
والرزق رزقان رزق الاجسام والأبدان بالأطعمة ونحوها، ورزق الارواح والمعارف بالإلهامات الصادقة وهو أسمى وأشرف الارزاق لان ثمرته المعرفة بالله الذي هو اساس السير والسلوك إلى الله سبحانه وتعالى.

وينبغي للعبد إن يعرف حقيقة وصف الرزاق وانه لا يستحقه إلا الله تعالى، فلا ينتظر الرزق إلا منه، ولا يتوكل إلا عليه، كما ذكر الإمام القشيري «من عرف إن الله هو الرزاق افرده بالقصد اليه» وكما جاء في الحديث الشريف عن الحبيب المصطفى صلى الله عليه وسلم قال «لو انكم تتوكلون على الله حق توكله لرزقكم كما يرزق الطير تغدو خماصا وتروح بطانا».

وعلى السالك إلى الله إذا اراد إن يفنى باسم الله الرزاق يجب عليه إن يعلم انه ربه لا شريك له في رزقه كما لا شريك له في خلقه، فهو يرجع اليه في جليل الرزق ودقيقه، وان يرضى بقسمة الرزاق الوهاب، فان توكل على الله حق توكله رزقه الله علما نافعا ولسانا مرشدا معلما ويدا منفقة، ويكون سببا لوصول الارزاق الشريفة إلى القلوب بأقواله واعماله.

وقد ورد اسم الله الرزاق في اماكن عديدة في القران الكريم ففي سورة طه «وأمر اهلك بالصلاة واصطبر عليها لا نسألك رزقا نحن نرزقك والعاقبة للتقوى» وفي سورة الذاريات «إن الله هو الرزاق ذو القوة المتين» وغيرها.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من قرأه قبل صلاة الفجر في كل ناحية من نواحي البيت عشر مرات وبدأ بالناحية اليمنى وهو مستقبل القبلة وسع الله عليه الرزق.
فاطلب من الله الرزاق أن يرزقك  علما هاديا، ولسانا مرشدا، ويدا منفقة متصدقة، فإن الله تعالى إذا أحب عبدا أكثر من حوائج الخلق إليه، وحبب إلى نفسه قضائها.

فيا الله انت الرزاق المتكفل بالعوالم الواسع الرحيم الذي يرزق المطيع والظالم، فالأرزاق بفضلك والبلاء بعدلك اللهم إن لك موائد تنزلها لأحبابك تغذي بها الارواح والقلوب فانزل علينا مائدة المعارف وسلمنا من المخاوف واجعلنا مظاهر توصل قوت الارواح لأحبابك واسبابا توصل قوت الاشباح لعبادك، واجعلنا مقبلين عليك بالأشواق إنك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: The Exalted says: “And in the heavens is your provision and that which you have been promised.”⁽¹⁾

• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, the Creator of provisions which He kindly sends to his creation. He is the One Who through His Grace provides every existent that which secures his material and bodily needs. He provides intellects with knowledge and souls with illuminations and bodies with food.

• He is the One that provides persons with the benefits of His kindness and souls His habitual Unveilings. As He, the Exalted, has said: “Allah is very kind with His servants. He provides whomsoever He wills and He is the Strong, the Almighty.”⁽²⁾

Provision is of two types: the provision for bodies with food and the like; and the provision for souls with true divine illumination. This latter is the higher and nobler provision since its fruit is the knowledge of Allah- it is the foundation for the travel to Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted.

It is necessary for the servant to know the reality of the description “the Provider” and that none deserves this title except Allah, the Exalted. Thus he will not expect provision except from Him. He will not trust in anybody except Him. As Imām Qushairī has said: “Whoever knows that Allah is the Provider will solely make Him his point of destination.” In a ḥadīth the Beloved, the Chosen One is reported to have said: “If you trust in Allah as He should be trusted He will provide for you like He provides for the birds. They leave in the morning hungry and return satiated.”⁽³⁾

The traveller to Allah, if he wishes to become absorbed in the name of Allah, the Provider, must know that His Lord has no partner in provisioning, just as He has no partner in His creation. He returns to Him whether the provisions are mighty or miniscule. He must content himself with the division of the Provider, the Bestower. If he trusts in Allah as He should be trusted, Allah will provide him with beneficial knowledge, a knowing, guiding tongue and a spending hand. He will become a means for the descent of spiritual provision into hearts through his sayings and acts.

The name of Allah, the Provider, occurs a number of times in the Noble Quran. In Sūrah Ṭā-Ḥā the Exalted says: “And enjoin ṣalāh on your family and be patient in offering them. We ask not of you for provision- We provide for you. And the good end is for the God-conscious.”⁽⁴⁾ And He also says: “Verily Allah is the Provider, the Owner of Power, and the Most Strong.”⁽⁵⁾

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whosoever recites it ten times prior to the ṣalāh of Fajr in each corner of his house- facing the Qiblah and beginning with the right side- Allah will widen his provision...

Accordingly, ask the Provider Most High to provide you with guiding knowledge, a couching tongue, and a charitable hand. When Allah loves one, He makes people come to him for their needs, and Allah creates a tendency for him to meet such needs.

Oh Allah, You are the guaranteed Provider of the worlds, the Extensive, the Merciful Who provides for both the obedient and unjust. Provision is from Your Grace and calamity from Your Justice. Oh Allah, with You are the needs sent down upon Your beloved ones. Through them You feed the souls and hearts. Send down upon us the provisions of divine illumination and preserve us from dangers. Make us manifestations by which arrive the sustenance for the souls of Your beloved ones. Make us a means by which descends nourishment for Your slaves. Make us submit to You with longing. Indeed You have power over all things.
refEnglish: (3)Aḥmad, Tirmithi, ibn Mājah and Ḥākim relating ‘Umar bin al-Khaṭṭāb, Allah be pleased with him.

descriptionArabic: الفتاح: اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى، قال تعالى «وهو الفتاح العليم» أي
• انه يفتح خزائن رحمته على عباده
• وبعنايته ينفتح كل مغلق، فهو الذي فتح قلوب المؤمنين بمعرفته وفتح للعاصين باب مغفرته

• وهو الذي بيده مفاتيح الغيب ومفاتيح الرزق، فهو الذي يرفع الحجاب عن قلوب اوليائه ويفتح لهم الابواب إلى ملكوت سمائه، وجمال كبريائه. فقال تعالى «وعنده مفاتح الغيب لا يعلمها إلا هو ويعلم ما في البر والبحر»، وقد جاء في سورة الفتح «إنا فتحنا لك فتحا مبينا». ويقول الإمام القشيري «إن الله تعالى فتاح لأنه يفتح على عباده ما انغلق عليهم من ابواب الرزق مما قصرت حيلهم عن فتحه» ومن ذلك قوله تعالى «فتحنا عليهم ابواب كل شيء»
• والفتح هو النصر، فقال تعالى في سورة الأنفال «إن تستفتحوا فقد جاءكم الفتح».
• والفتح هو الحكم، فقال تعالى في سورة الأعراف «ربنا افتح بيننا وبين قومنا بالحق وانت خير الفاتحين»
وقد ورد اسم الله الفتاح في عدة مواضع في القرآن.

ويرى العارف بالله إن السالك إلى الله متى توجه إلى الله بكليته فتح له ابواب السماء واستجاب له الدعاء وكاشفه بما يستحقه من الاسرار وجعله من المقربين الاخيار، فعلى السالك إلى الله اذا اراد إن يكون له قبس من نور اسم الله الفتاح فعليه بالاقتداء برسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وان يكون دائما وابدا راضيا بما جرى به قضاء ربه دائم التقرب لحصول فضله، مستديم التطلع لنيل كرم الحق سبحانه وتعالى عليه.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من قرأه عقب صلاة الفجر احدى وسبعين مرة واضعا يده على صدره طهر قلبه وتنور سره وتيسر امره وقضيت حاجاته بإذن الله.
ومن علم بأن الله هو الفتاح لكل أبواب اليسر لا يتعلق قلبه بغيره، ولا يفكر إلا فيه .

فيا رباه ويا غوثاه انت الفتاح الذي تفتتح العباد بكرمك، وتمدهم بوافر نعمك، تفتح القلوب فتطلعها على اسرار الغيوب، تفتح ابواب السماء بقبول الدعاء، فافتح لنا ابواب رحمتك، وعلمنا ما لم نكن نعلم من سر الجسم والروح فانت صاحب المدد والفتوح انك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: The Opener is one of the Grand Names of Allah. Allah, the Exalted, says: “He is the Opener, the All Knowing.”⁽¹⁾ That is,
• He opens the treasures of His mercy to His servants;
• And by His grace, every closed avenue is opened. He is the One Who opens the hearts of the believers to His Knowledge and opens the door of forgiveness to sinners.

• He is the One in Whose Hand are the keys to the Unseen. He is the One Who lifts the veil off the heart of His wards. He opens for them the doors to His heavenly Kingdom and the beauty of His grandeur. The Most High declares that “with Him are the opening keys to the Unseen; none knows them but He. And He knows what is in the land and in the sea”⁽²⁾; In Sūrah al-Fatḥ He states “Indeed We have opened for you a clear Victory.”⁽³⁾ Imām Qushairī says: “Allah, the Exalted, is the Opener because He opens to His servants what was closed to them of the doors of providence- the opening of which their own strength is inadequate.” Regarding this, the Exalted says: “We have opened to them the doors to everything.”⁽⁴⁾
• Opening refers also to victory. The Most High declares “If you ask for an opening victory, then the opening has come”⁽⁵⁾.
• Opening further refers to judgement. The Most High declares “Oh our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the best Judge”⁽⁶⁾;
The name, the Opener, occurs a number of times in the Quran: and   as well as in other places.

The Knower of Allah sees that the traveller to Allah, when he turns towards Allah, the doors of the heavens are opened to him and his supplication is answered. He reveals to him what he merits of the mysteries and makes him of the chosen near ones. The traveller to Allah must- if he wishes to gain a portion of the light emanating from Allah’s name, the Opener- must follow the Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him and at all times be pleased with what Allah has destined for him. He must anticipate the obtaining of Allah’s favour, incessantly striving to acquire the munificence of Allah upon him.

Of the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever recites it 70 times after the Fajr prayer-putting his hand on his chest- his heart will be cleansed, his secret illuminated, his affairs facilitated and his needs fulfilled, by the permission of Allah.
Whoever knows that it is Allah Who is the Opener of all doors of ease will not attach his heart to any other, and not even think of others.

Oh Lord! Oh Help! You are the Opener that conquers servants by Your liberality, and extends to them Your ample favours, You open hearts and acquaint them with the secrets of the Unseen, You open the doors of the heavens by the acceptance of supplication- open for us the doors of Your mercy, and inform us of that we do not know of the secrets of the body and the soul. For You are the granter of help and victory. Indeed You have power over all things.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «ربنا تقبل منا انك انت السميع العليم» فالعليم لفظ مشتق من العلم،
• وهو الذي يعلم تفاصيل الامور ودقائق الاشياء، وخفايا الضمائر والنفوس،
• وهو الذي احاط بكل شيء علما فهو الذي يعلم ما كان وما يكون عنده علم الغيب وعلم الساعة ويعلم ما في الارحام ويعلم نزول الغيث ويعلم ما تكسب كل نفس ويعلم باي ارض تموت.
وهناك فرق كبير بين علم الله وعلم العباد:
• فعلم الله كثير واسع اما علم العبد فهو قليل محصور،
• علم الله غير مستفاد من الاشياء، بل الاشياء مستفادة منه،
• علم العبد لا يبلغ الغاية التي ليس وراءها غاية بخلاف علم الله كما قال سبحانه وتعالى في سورة فاطر «إن الله عالم غيب السماوات والارض».
وفي رواية إن جبريل عليه السلام قال لخليل الرحمن ابراهيم وهو في محنته: هل لك حاجة، فأجاب ابراهيم، اما اليك فلا، فقال له جبريل، فأسأل الله تعالى، فقال ابراهيم: حسبي في سؤالي علمه بحالي يغني عن سؤالي.
ومن اقوال الصالحين «من عرف أن الله عليم بحاله صبر على بليته وشكر على عطيته واعتذر عن قبيح خطيئته».
وقد جاء اسم الله العليم اكثر من مائة وخمسين مرة في القرآن الكريم ففي سورة الانفال «وتوكل على الله انه هو السميع العليم» وفي سورة الانعام «وله ما سكن في الليل والنهار وهو السميع العليم» وغيرها.

فالعبد الموفق اذا اراد إن يتجلى الحق عليه باسم الله العليم عليه إن يتقي الله ويخشاه في السر والعلانية وان يجتهد بذكر الله ويخلي قلبه من الغش والحسد وحب الدنيا عندئذ يشرق الحق على قلبه نور اسم الله العليم فيصبح من الراسخين في العلم كما قال تعالى «انما يخشى الله من عباده العلماء» والعلماء هنا هم اهل الخشية، اللهم اجعلنا منهم يا رب الارباب.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من ذكره بصيغة يا عالم الغيب والشهادة بعد كل صلاة مائة مرة أصبح من اهل الكشف الإيماني وظهرت على لسانه الحكم الالهية بإذنه تعالى.

ومن وضع نصب عينيه علم الحق تعالى بخفايا الأمور ودقائقها، صبر على بليته، وشكره على عطيته. ومن أكثر من ذكره رزقه الله الفهوم الربانية، والعلوم اللدنية، وظهرت على لسانه الحكم الإلهية، والله أعلم بالصواب، والهادي إلى طريق الرشاد .

فيا إلهي، انت العليم بالأكوان قبل بروزها بالشهود، الخبير بدقائق الاشياء قبل اتصافها بالوجود، الخلائق جهال إلا من علمته، والعباد حيارى إلا من هديته فأشرق يا الله على قلوبنا انوار اسمك العليم حتى تنكشف لنا حقيقتنا في العلم القديم وننجو من الغرور بمحض الكرم يا أكرم الاكرمين.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “Our Lord! Accept (this service) from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.”⁽¹⁾ The word the Knowing is derived from knowledge.
• He is the One Who knows the details of matters and the most subtle aspects of things, and what is concealed in the hearts and selves.

• He is the One Who encompasses all things through His Knowledge and knows what was and what will be. With Him is the knowledge of the Unseen and the knowledge of the Hour. He knows what is in the wombs, when rain will descend, what every soul will earn and where it will die.
There is a great difference between Allah’s knowledge and what His slaves know.
• A slave’s knowledge is paltry and limited compared to the vastness and extent of Allah’s knowledge.
• The Knowledge of Allah is not learnt from things- but things are learnt from Him.

• The knowledge of a slave cannot reach an extent beyond which there cannot be an extent- unlike the Knowledge of Allah. As Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, says in Sūrah Fāṭir: “Allah is indeed the Knower of the Unseen, the heavens and the earth.”⁽²⁾

It is narrated that Jibrīl, upon whom be peace, asked the Lover of Allah, Ibrāhīm, when the latter was being tested “Do you need anything?” Ibrāhīm answered: “From you-no.” Jibrīl said: “Then ask Allah, the Exalted.” Ibrāhīm replied: “Sufficient for me is His Knowledge of my condition- I have no need to ask Him.”
One of the righteous said: “Whoever is aware that Allah knows his condition, is patient in what He tries him with, thankful for what He gives him, and pleads for his sins.”

Allah’s name, the Knower, occurs more than 150 times in the Noble Quran. Sūrah Anfāl says: “And trust in Allah-He is the Hearing, the Seeing.”⁽³⁾ Sūrah al-An‘ām says: “And to Him belongs whatever exists during the night and the day. He is the All-hearing, the All-Knowing.”⁽⁴⁾ And so forth.

The successful slave, if He wants the Truth to be revealed to him through Allah’s name, the Knowing, must fear and be conscious of Allah in secret and in public. He must strive in remembering Allah and empty his heart of deception, envy and the love of this world. Then Allah will let his heart radiate with the light of Allah’s name, the Knower, and he will become one of those deeply-grounded in knowledge. As Allah, the Exalted says: “It is only those of knowledge among His slaves that fear Him.”⁽⁵⁾

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever recites it as follows: “Oh Knower of the Unseen and the Witnessed” a hundred times after every ṣalāh will become one of the people of true disclosure and his tongue will communicate divine wisdom, by the permission of Allah.

Whoever knows for sure that Allah knows all subtle things will be patient on His trials and thankful for His graces. Whoever repeats this name, Allah will grant him Divine understanding and knowledge, and wisdom will overflow from his tongue. Allah knows best what is right, and He is the Guide for the right way.

Oh Allah, You are the Knower of existents before their manifestation in the world of creation. You are aware of the most subtle aspects of things prior to their being described by existence. People are ignorant except those whom You have given knowledge. People are floundering except those whom You have guided. Let the lights of Your name, the Knowing, radiate in our hearts so that our reality in the eternal knowledge is disclosed to us and we are saved from delusion solely through Your generosity, oh Most Generous of those who show generosity.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «والله يقبض ويبسط واليه ترجعون» فالقابض هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى
• فهو الذي يكاشفنا بجلاله وهو الذي يخوفنا من عذابه وحجابه،
• وهو الذي يقبض النفوس بقهره والارواح بعدله والارزاق بحكمته، وهو مخرج الارواح من الاشباح عند الموت.

والقبض نعمة من الله تعالى على عباده، لأنهم يستحضرون معنى اسم القابض فيتقربون منه، فاذا قبض الحق الرزق عن انسان توجه بكليته إلى الله يسأله ويستعطفه، والسالك إلى الله ما بين قبض وبسط فإذا تجلى عليه الحق بالبسط عمه سرور ورضا، ولكن إذا حصل معه شيء من القبض توجه لخالقه سأله إن يكشف عنه هذا القبض، وغالبا يكون سبب القبض هو الغفلة والبعد عن الله، فاجعل يا سالك قلبك عامرا بذكر الله في كل نفس من انفاسك.

وقد ورد اسم القابض في آيات كثيرة منها في سورة الزمر «وما قدروا الله حق قدره والارض جميعا قبضته يوم القيامة والسماوات مطويات بيمينه سبحانه وتعالى عما يشركون» وفي سورة الفرقان «ثم قبضناه الينا قبضا يسيرا» وغيرها.
وقد سئل الجنيد على ماذا يتأسف المحب؟ فأجاب: على زمان بسط اورث قبضا، او زمان انس اورث وحشة.

والعارف بالله الذي تخلق بأنوار هذا الاسم الشريف، قد قبض نفسه بزمام الجهاد فلا يجعلها تمرح وتفرح بغيد الله، وقبض اهل المعاصي عن إن يخالفوا اوامر مولاهم، وقبض لسانه عن إن يتكلم في غيبة او نميمة ونحوها، وقبض جميع جوارحه بمراقبة مولاه صاحب العزة الجبروتية حتى عرف نور القابض في نفسه.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من كتبه اربعين يوما على اربعين لقمة من الخبز واكل كل يوم لقمة لم يحس بألم الجوع، ومن اتخذه وردا لإهلاك عدو أو ظالم أهلكه الله بإذنه تعالى.

إلهي انت القابض على نواصي العباد المتصرف في القلوب والارواح بسبب الاستعداد، القبض في الظاهر بلية ولكنه عين العطية، فلولا القبض ما تأدب معك العبد لأنك حكيم تفعل ما تريد تقبض الارزاق فتقبل العباد عليك بالتوبة، وتقبض القلوب فتتأدب معك النفوس من جلال الهيبة فامنحنا يا الله قوة نقبض بها على زمام أنفسنا وامنحنا همة نقبض بها على كل من اتصل بنا فيكون قائما بطاعتك إنك على كل شيء قدير
descriptionEnglish: Allah Most High declares “Allah contracts and expands and to Him you return.”⁽¹⁾ The Contractor is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One Who discloses to us through His Glory and the one Who makes us fear His punishment and His Veil.
• He is the One Who constricts selves by His compulsion, constricts souls by His justice, and constricts sustenance by His Wisdom. He is the One Who extracts souls from bodies at death.

Contraction is a favour of Allah to His servants since they call to mind the name, the Constrictor, and come close to Him. If Allah, the Truth, contracts the sustenance of a person the latter turns towards Allah with his whole being, asking Him, imploring Him. The traveller to Allah is one who is between the state of contraction and expansion. If Allah, the Truth discloses Himself to him through expansion he should be diffused with happiness and contentment. But if he finds something therein of contraction he should turn towards His Creator and ask Him to remove it from him. In the majority of cases the cause of contraction is forgetfulness and distance from Allah. So, oh traveller, make Your heart filled with the remembrance of Allah with every breath in breathing.

The name, the Constrictor, occurs many times in the Quran- such as, for example, in Sūrah Zumar “They made not a just estimate of Allah such as is due to Him. And on the Day of Judgment the whole of the earth will be contracted within His Hand and the heavens will be rolled up in His Right Hand. Glorified be He, and He is Exalted above that which they associate as partners with Him.”⁽²⁾ And in Sūrah Furqan: “Then We withdraw it to us- a gradual, concealed contraction.”⁽³⁾
Imam Junayd was asked as to what grieves the lover? He replied: “When in a state of expansion he draws down contraction, or when in a state of intimacy he draws down loneliness.”

The Knower of Allah, who is moulded by lights of this noble name is the One Who has contracted his self through with the rein of self-sacrifice and he does not let it delight in that which is other than Allah. Those who are disobedient should withdraw from that which goes against the commands of their Protector. They should withdraw their tongue from backbiting, slander and so forth. They should constrict their limbs out of fear of their Protector, the Possessor of Honour and Might, until they know the light of the Constrictor in themselves.

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever, for forty days, writes it on forty pieces of bread and eats one of those pieces of bread each day, will not experience the pain of hunger. Whoever takes it as a litany for the destruction of an enemy or oppressor, Allah will destroy the latter, with His permission.

Oh God, You are the Contactor of the forelocks of the slaves, overturning hearts and souls in order to incline them [to You]. Contraction in its outward aspect is a calamity but in reality it is a gift. For had there not been contraction the slave would not have behaved with respect towards You. Because You are the Wise, the Doer of what You want, You contract sustenance and then the slave comes before You in repentance. You contract hearts and then lower selves behave with respect towards You due to the Glory of Your Godhood. Oh Allah, grant us strength by which we contract our lower selves, reining them in. Grant us determination by which we contract everyone who comes to us in order that they become steadfast in Your obedience. Indeed You have power over all things.

descriptionArabic: الباسط هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى، قال تعالى «إن ربك يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء ويقدر»
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى موسع الارزاق لمن شاء من عباده
• وهو الذي ينشر الارواح في الاجساد عند الحياة،
• ويبسطها في الاشباح عند البعث لعرض الأعمال
• وهو باسط القلوب أي موسعها بالعلم والمعرفة.
وقد ورد اسم الباسط في عدة اماكن في القرآن الكريم منها في سورة الشورى «يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء ويقدر انه بكل شيء عليم» وفي سورة نوح «والله جعل لكم الارض بساطا» وغيرها.

والعارف بالله الذي تذوق معاني نور اسمه القابض والباسط هو الذي الهم بدائع الحكم واولي جوامع الكلم، فتارة يبسط قلوب مريديه واخوانه بما يذكرهم من آلاء الله ونعمه وتارة يقبضها بما يخوفهم من جلال الله وكبريائه وفنون عذابه وبلائه.
والسالك إلى الله ما بين قبض وبسط، فان غلب على قلبه الخوف كان من اهل القبض، وان غلب على قلبه الرجاء كان من اهل البسط.
وقد ذكر بعض العارفين إن الحق سبحانه وتعالى لا يوصف بالقبض دون البسط، يعني انه لا يوسف بالحرمان دون العطاء، ولا بالعطاء دون الحرمان.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من لازم على ذكره بعد صلاة الضحى عشر مرات رزقه الله بسط الرزق والعلم واورثه الغنى بإذنه تعالى.
من ذكره وكان صاحب همة صادقة بسط الله رزقه، وأحيا قلبه، وأزال غمه وهمه، وأحبه كل من يراه.

فيا الله انت الباسط للقلوب بشهودك، والباسط للأجسام بجودك، تجلى لنا بنور اسمك الباسط حتى تظهر انوار هذا الاسم الشريف على جوارحنا، فمن رآنا انبسط وانشرح صدره ومن واجهنا عمه الفرح، واجعل لساننا منبسطا لعبادك بلطف الكلام واجعل يا الله قلبنا في بسط وهيام، حتى لو فرق ما عندنا من البسط والسرور لوسع العالم وشرح الصدور إنك على كل شيء قدير
descriptionEnglish: The Expander is one of the Grand Names of Allah. Allah, the Exalted, says: “Allah expands and constricts provision for whom He wills.”⁽¹⁾ Thus
• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, expands provisions for whom He wills among His servants.
• He is the One Who diffuses souls into bodies during life and
• Expands them in persons at Resurrection in order to manifest deeds.
• He is the expander of hearts- that is, He widens them with knowledge and gnosis.

The name, the Expander, occurs in a number of places in the Noble Quran. For example, in Sūrah ash-Shūrā it says: “Allah expands and constricts provision for whom He wills. Indeed He has knowledge of all things.”⁽²⁾ And in Sūrah Nūḥ it says: “Allah has made the earth expansive for you.”⁽³⁾ And so forth.

The Knower of Allah is the one who has tasted the meanings of the two Names, the Contracting and the Expander. He is the one inspired with peerless wisdom and the entrusted with the comprehensive word. At times the hearts of his disciples and brothers expand due to what he mentions to them of the blessings and favours of Allah and sometimes these hearts contract due to the fear he instils in them concerning the Majesty and Self Glory of Allah and His various punishments and tests.
The traveller to Allah is between a state of expansion and contraction. If fear dominates his heart he is of the people of contraction. If hope dominates his heart he is of the people of expansion.

One of the knowers of Allah said that Allah, the truth, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, cannot be described in terms of contraction without the ascription of expansion. That is, He cannot be described in terms of withholding without the ascription of giving, nor can He be described in terms of giving without the ascription of withholding.

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever imposes upon himself to recite it ten times after the Salāh of Duḥā, Allah will grant him expansive provisions and knowledge and cause him to be independent of others, by the permission of Allah.
Whoever repeats this name diligently, Allah will bless his provisions, enliven his heart, removes his concerns, and make him beloved to those who see him.

Oh Allah, You are the Expander of hearts by Your Witnessing, and You are the Expander of bodies by Your liberality. Illuminate for us the lights of Your name, the Expander, so that the lights of this noble name are manifested in our limbs. So that whoever sees us- such a one’s chest will be made expansive and whoever turns towards us will be diffused with happiness. Make our tongue expansive to Your slaves through kind words. And make our hearts-oh Allah- expansive and full of longing so that if what is with us of expansion and happiness were to be dispersed, it would enrich the universe and expand chests. Indeed You have power over all things.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى في سورة الواقعة «خافضة رافعة» وجاء ايضا في سورة الحجر «واخفض جناحك للمؤمنين»
فالخافض اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي يخفض بالإذلال من تعاظم وتكبر
• وهو الواضع لمن عصاه، والمذل لمن غضب عليه يخفض اعداؤه بالإبعاد ويخفض ايضا كل خارج عن شريعته، ويرفع اولياؤه واحبابه «اللهم ارفعنا بطاعتك ولا تخفضنا بمعصيتك»

وعلى السالك إلى الله إذا اراد إن ينال حظا من اسم الله الخافض عليه إن يخفض نفسه فيراها اقل من جميع العباد، ومتى سلم من وساوس نفسه الامارة بالسوء نجا وفاز برضا الله سبحانه وتعالى لان النفس هي من احدى القواطع التي تقطع السالك عن السير والسلوك إلى الله تعالى وكما جاء في الحديث النبوي الشريف «إن الله يخفض القسط ويرفعه» أي يرفع العدل فيعليه على الجود واهله، ومرة يخفض العدل فيظهر اهل الجور على العدل ابتلاء لخلقه، وهذا في الدنيا والعاقبة للمتقين فسبحان من بيده ملكوت كل شيء.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من قرأه في اليوم سبعين الف مرة في مجلس واحد وذكر من اراد من اعدائه كفاه الله شرهم ونصره عليهم.

وقد ورد اسم الله الخافض مرات عديدة في القرآن الكريم نذكر منها في سورة الشعراء «واخفض جناحك لمن اتبعك من المؤمنين» وفي سورة الاسراء «واخفض لهما جناح الذل من الرحمة» ويتكلم القشيري عن اسم الله الخافض بقوله: انه سبحانه وتعالى يرفع من يشاء بإنعامه ويخفض من يشاء بانتقامه، فمن زكى نفسه وطهرها فهو المرفوع حقيقة ومن دس نفسه واسرته شهواته وهواه فهو المخذول حقيقة، اللهم اعنا على شهوات أنفسنا يا أكرم الاكرمين.

الهي انت الخافض للجبارين بقهرك، المذل للمتكبرين بجبروتك، المتعالي العلي الكبير اسالك يا ربي إن تمنحني قوة جبروتية اخفض بها كل جبار عنيد واصغر بها نفسي وشيطاني المريد اتواضع بها لوالدي واتذلل بها لكل مسلم فيكون لي أوفر حظ من نور هذا الاسم الشريف انك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says in Sūrah Wāqi‘ah: “Abasing and Elevating.”⁽¹⁾ In Sūrah Al Ḥijr Allah ordered His Prophet to “lower your wings before the believers.”⁽²⁾
The Abaser/ the One Who lowers is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One who debases the arrogant and proud and humiliates those who disobey Him.

• He debases whosoever angers Him and abases His enemies by keeping them far from Him. He also abases whoever leaves His revealed Law (Sharī‘ah). He elevates His wards and beloved ones- Oh Allah, elevate us through obedience to You and do not abase us by being disobedient to You.

The traveller to Allah- if he wishes to obtain a portion of treasure from Allah’s name, the Abaser/the One Who lowers- must abase his lower self and regard himself the lowest of creation. When he becomes preserved from the whisperings of the self that prompts to evil, then he is saved and successful through the pleasure of Allah, the Exalted. This is since the lower self is one of the obstacles that cut off a traveller’s journey to Allah, the Exalted. A Prophetic ḥadīth says: “Allah abases fairness or elevates it.”⁽³⁾ That is He elevates justice and overwhelms the unjust and, then again, He abases justice and the unjust are granted victory over justice, afflicting His creation. This is in this world- and the Hereafter is for those who fear Allah, Glory be to Him in Whose Hand is the Dominion over all things.

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever recites it 17000 times in one gathering in the day- mentioning the enemies he wishes to overcome- Allah will save him from their evil and grant him victory over them.

The name of Allah, the Abaser/the One Who lowers, occurs a number of times in the noble Quran- for instance, in Sūrah Shu‘arā “And lower your wing [of kindness and mercy] to the believers who follow you”⁽⁴⁾ and in Sūrah Isrā: “And lower unto them the wing of submission and mercy ”⁽⁵⁾ Qushairī says about the name of Allah, the Abaser/the One Who lowers: “Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, elevates whomsoever He wills through His favour and abases whomsoever He wills through His vengeance. So whosoever purifies his self is in reality elevated and whosoever engages his self in schemes and is kept prisoner by his passions is in reality forsaken. Oh Allah, assist us against the desires of our self, oh Most merciful of those who show mercy.

Oh Allah, You are the Abaser of tyrants through Your compelling, the Debaser of the proud through Your Might, the Sublime, the Exalted, the Great. I ask You, my Lord, that You grant me strength and might that I may thereby abase every obstinate tyrant and that I may thereby belittle myself and my devil. I desire thereby to humble myself to my father and to lower myself thereby to every Muslim. Thus let be granted a vast treasure from the light of this noble name. Indeed You have power over all things.
refEnglish: (3)Muslim and Ibn Mājah relating Abū Mūsā, Allah be pleased with him.

descriptionArabic: الرافع هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي يرفع الاولياء فينصرهم على الاعداء ويرفع الصالحين إلى اعلى عليين، ويرفع المؤمنين بالإسعاد ويرفع من تولاه حقا وعدلا.

وهو الذي رفع مقام الاولياء في الحياة بخضوع القلوب لهم، ونصرهم على اعدائهم كما جاء في قوله سبحانه وتعالى «إلا إن اولياء الله لا خوف عليهم ولا هم يحزنون، الذين امنوا وكانوا يتقون، لهم البشرى في الحياة الدنيا وفي الاخرة لا تبديل لكلمات الله ذلك هو الفوز العظيم».
وهو سبحانه مدبر شؤون خلقه، يرفع من تولاه إلى افق المقربين ويخفض من عصاه إلى اسفل السافلين كما جاء في قوله تعالى «ورفعنا بعضهم فوق بعض درجات».

وقد وردت مادة الرفع والرافع في القرآن المجيد في عدة مواضع ففي سورة الرحمن «والسماء رفعها ووضع الميزان» وفي سورة الانعام «وتلك حجتنا آتيناها ابراهيم على قومه نرفع درجات من نشاء إن ربك حكيم عليم» وفي سورة مريم «واذكر في الكتاب ادريس انه كان صديقا نبيا ورفعناه مكانا عليا».

والسالك إلى الله الذي تجلى له الحق بنور اسمه الرافع فقد رفعه هذا النور إلى العلى الاعلى فصار مرتفعا عن الاكوان لا ينظر إلى ما في ايدي الناس، قد رفع الله همته إن تكون عبادته لغير الله ورفع قدر نفسه فلا يجعل لذته في الشهوات الفانية، واعظم انسان رفعه الله هو سيد الاكوان رسول الله عليه الصلاة والسلام، فعليك الاقتداء به يا سالك لان الاقتداء بالكامل يكمل.
ويتحقق حظ العبد من فيض هذين الاسمين بفضل الله بان يرفع الحق ويخفض الباطل فيعادي اعداء الله ليخفضهم ويوالي اولياء الله ليرفعهم وبان يرفع جانب الروح ويخفض جانب النفس.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من قرأه في نصف الليل او النهار اصطفاه الله تعالى من خلقه ويسر امره واغناه بإذنه تعالى.

فيا رباه تجليت باسمك الرافع فرفعت قدرا من انبيائك واولياءك فأظهرت لهم المعجزات وابرزت لهم الكرامات فعظمتهم القلوب ورفعت اعمالهم اليك بالقلوب ورفعت ارواحهم اليك بالوصول فلم يطلبوا سواك لان عيون ارواحهم تراك فاجعلنا رافعين مرفوعين بفضلك يا رافع يا معين إنك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: The Elevator is one of the Grand Names of Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted-

• He Who elevates His wards and grants them victory over the enemies, and elevates the righteous to the highest of the uppermost heavens. He elevates the believers with divine help and felicity and elevates truly and justly whosoever puts their trust in Him.

He is the one who raises the stations of His wards in this life through the submission of their hearts and He grants them victory over their enemies. As He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, says: “There is no fear upon the wards of Allah nor do they grieve. Those who believed and feared Allah much. To them are glad tidings in the life of this world and the Hereafter. There is no change in the words of Allah. That is the supreme success.”⁽¹⁾

He, Glory be to Him, who arranges the affairs of His creation. He elevates those who trust in Him to the heights of the Near ones and abases those who disobey Him to the lowest of the low. As the Quran says: “We have elevated some of them above others.”⁽²⁾

The subject of elevating and the Elevator occurs in the Quran in a number of places: in Sūrah Raḥmān: “He has elevated the sky and He has set up the Scale of justice”⁽³⁾, in Sūrah al-An‘ām: “And that was Our proof which We gave Ibrāhīm against his people. We raise the degrees of whom We will- indeed Your Lord is the Wise, the All-Knowing.”⁽⁴⁾ , in Sūrah Maryam: “And mention in the Book Idrīs. Indeed He was a Truthful one, a Prophet. And We elevated him to an exalted station.”⁽⁵⁾

The traveller to Allah to whom the Truth is disclosed through the light of His name, the Elevator- this light elevates him to the Most Exalted and he passes away from the creation and does not look at what is in people’s hands. Allah elevates his determination so that his worship is for none but Allah and He elevates the rank of his self so that he does not feel a taste for passing passions. The greatest of human beings that Allah has elevated is the Master of creation, the Messenger of Allah, the Salutations and Peace be upon him. Thus, oh traveller, you must follow him since following perfection brings perfection.

The slave will [then] realise treasure issuing forth from both these Names by the grace of Allah- in that he elevates the truth and debases falsehood, and thus he at war with the enemies of Allah and assists the wards of Allah in order to elevate them. And in that he elevates the portion of the soul and debases the portion of the lower self.

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever recites it at midday or midnight Allah, the Exalted, will make him one of the select in His creation, facilitate his affair and make him independent, by the permission of Allah.

Oh Lord, You have manifested through Your name, the Elevator, and thus You have elevated the degrees of Your Prophets and Your men. You have manifested Prophetic and saintly miracles in them and thus made hearts magnify them and You have elevated their works to You through hearts and elevated their souls to You through arrival at You. They did not ask for anything but You because the eyes of their souls only see You. Make us of those who ascend and are elevated through Your abundant favour, oh Elevator, oh Help- indeed You have power over all things.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى في سورة المنافقون «ولله العزة ولرسوله وللمؤمنين ولكن المنافقين لا يعلمون».
المعز هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• فهو الذي يهب العز لمن يشاء من عباده،
• وهو الذي أعز اولياءه فضلا بعصمته وغفر لهم برحمته واحلهم دار كرامته ثم اكرمهم برؤيته ومشاهدته،
• فهو الذي يعز الأنبياء بالعصمة والنصر ويرفع المطبع ولو كان فقيرا، ويرفع المتقي ولو كان عبدا حبشيا.
كما يقول بعض العلماء، من اطاع الله واجتنب معاصيه اعزه الله تعالى فليس هناك طاعة إلا والعز معها وليس هناك معصية إلا والذل معها.
وكما قال الإمام زين العابدين علي بن الحسين رضي الله عنه «من أراد عزا بلا عشيرة وهيبة بلا سلطان وغنى بلا فقر فليخرج من ذل المعصية إلى عز الطاعة» اللهم انقلنا من ذل معصيتك إلى عز طاعتك.

وقد اقترن اسم الله العزيز مع العديد من اسماء الله الحسنى في القران الكريم ففي سورة هود اقترن اسم العزيز باسم القوي «إن ربك هو القوي العزيز» وفي سورة الشعراء فقد اقترن اسم العزيز باسم الرحيم «وان ربك لهو العزيز الرحيم» وفي سورة غافر اقترن اسم العزيز باسم الله العليم «حم، تنزيل الكتاب من الله العزيز العليم» وغيرها.

والعارف بالله الذي اشرق الحق على قلبه نور اسمه العزيز هو الذي رفع الحق عن قلبه الحجاب حتى شاهد جمال حضرته وامده الحق بالقوة والتأييد واعزه بالكشف والبقاء واهله لمقامات النجوى ليصبح من اهل القرب والوصال فكل نور وكشف حجاب هو بفضل الطاعة والقرب منه سبحانه وتعالى والسالك إلى الله اذا اراد يفنى في هذا الاسم الشريف فعليه إن يعز رسول الله ويعز العارفين في عصره ويتقرب اليهم فيحترمهم ويتواضع لهم ويبذل كل شيء في رضاهم.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم انه من ذكر هذا الاسم ليلة الجمعة او الاثنين بعد صلاة المغرب حصلت له الهيبة في قلوب الخلائق وصار لا يخاف إلا من الله.
من داوم على ذكره جعله الله في مركز العزة، وأودع في قلوب الناس هيبته .

فيا رباه تجلى لنا باسمك المعز فاعزنا حتى لا يرانا مخلوق إلا ويرى تاج عزك على رؤوسنا فيخضع لك يا معز، فانت المعز لأحبابك تكاشفهم بالجمال فيفرون اليك وتلاطفهم بالحنان فيركنون اليك فلا تجعل لنا عزا سواك يا أكرم الاكرمين وصلى الله على سيدنا محمد وعلى آله وصحبه وسلم.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says in Sūrah al-Munāfiqūn: “To Allah belongs dignity, and to His Messenger and to the believers- but the hypocrites do not know.”⁽¹⁾
The Dignifier is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One Who bestows honour and dignity  upon whomsoever He wills among His servants.

• He is the One Who abundantly bestows honour upon His wards through His safeguarding of them, Who forgives them through His mercy, Who establishes them in the House of Grace and Generosity and then honours them with His vision and witnessing of realities.
• He is the One Who honours and dignifies the Prophets with sinlessness and victory. He elevates the obedient and God-conscious- be they poor or slaves.

One of the scholars have said: “Whoever obeys Allah and steers clear of disobedience will be honoured by Allah, the Exalted. There is no obedience except accompanied by honour and dignity and there is no disobedience except accompanied by debasement.”

Imām Zayn ul ‘Ābidīn ibn Hussain said: “Whoever desires honour without kinsfolk, awe without authority and richness beyond need let him proceed from the debasement of disobedience to the dignity of obedience.” Oh Allah, let us be transported from the debasement of disobedience to You to the honour of obedience to You.”

In the Quran, the name of Allah, the Owner of Honour, is joined to a number of Allah’s other Grand Names. In Sūrah Hūd the name “The Owner of Honour” is joined to the name, the Strong: “Indeed Your Lord is the Owner of Honour, the Strong.”⁽²⁾ In Sūrah Shu‘arā it is joined to the name, the Merciful: “Indeed Your Lord is the Owner of Honour, the Merciful.”⁽³⁾ In Sūrah Ghāfir it is joined to the name, the Knowing: “The Revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Knowing.”⁽⁴⁾ And so forth.

The Knower of God – whose heart Allah radiates with the light of this Name- is the one from whose heart the veil has been lifted and thus he witnesses the beauty of His Presence. Allah extends to him strength and assistance and honours him with disclosure and the state of permanence in Allah. He exults him with elevated states so that he becomes of the people of nearness and arrival [to Allah’s presence]. Every light and unveiling through the grace of obedience and nearness is from Him. The traveller to Allah, if he wishes to become absorbed in this noble name, must honour the Messenger of Allah and honour the Knowers of Allah of his time. He must seek to be near them, respect and submit to them and sacrifice everything to please them.
Among the special qualities of this name is that whoever recites this name Sunday nights or Thursday nights after Maghreb prayers, creation will become awed of him and he will fear none but Allah.
Whoever repeats this name, Allah will elevate him to the centre of honour, and will make his respect in the heart of people.

Oh Lord, illuminate and honour us through Your name, the One Who gives Honour, so that creation does not see us  except that they see the Crown of Your honour upon our heads and thus submit to You- oh Giver of Honour! For You are the Giver of Honour to Your beloved ones. You disclose Yourself to them through beauty and  so they flee to You. You show affectionate kindness to them and so they resort to You. So do not give us honour that is from other than You, oh Most Generous of those who show generosity. The Salutations and Peace of Allah be upon our Master Muhammad, his Family and Companions.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «وتعز من تشاء وتذل من تشاء» المذل هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى يلحق الذل بمن شاء من عباده العصاة
• وهو الذي اذل اعداءه عدلا بعصيانهم ومخالفتهم واهانهم وطردهم وادخلهم دار عقوبته.

والذل يكون بأنواع وألوان، فقد يذل الله سبحانه وتعالى الإنسان الجبار بالمرض او بالمال او بطريق الاحتياج إلى سواه، اللهم اخرجنا من ذل المعصية إلى عز الطاعة ولا تذلنا لاحد من خلقك الطغاة وتوجنا بتاج عزك، وقد قال أحد العارفين ما اعز الله عبدا بمثل ما يذله على ذل نفسه وما اذل الله عبدا بمثل ما يرده إلى توهم عزه.

وقد ورد اسم الله المذل في القرآن المجيد في عدة مواضع نذكر منها في سورة الاعراف :«إن الذين اتخذوا العجل سينالهم غضب من ربهم وذلة في الحياة الدنيا» وفي سورة المجادلة «إن الذين يحادون الله ورسوله اولئك في الاذلين» وغيرها.

والسالك إلى الله حتى يصبح في مراتب كمال الروح يجب عليه إن يعرف الحق لذاته، والخير لأجل العمل به فاذا توجه في هذا الطريق أصبح مستغرقا في شهود انوار الربوبية منقطع الفكر عن كل ما سوى الله، اذل نفسه لعباد الله المؤمنين وقمع شهواته في سبيل رضا الحق سبحانه وتعالى فهذا هو الاعزاز المطلق، وان كان عكس ذلك فهو الاذلال المطلق.
وقد قال بعضهم: ما اعز الله عبدا بمثل ما يذله على ذل نفسه، وما اذل الله عبدا بمثل ما يشغله بعز نفسه لذلك السالك إلى الله اذا اذل نفسه اصبح عزيزا، واذا اعز نفسه اصبح ذليلا.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من دعا بهذا الدعاء رفع الله قدره ونال ما يريد من قمع المتمردين والجبارين.

اللهم انت المعز الذي لا يشابه عزك عزة كل عزيز ولا يصل إلى كبريائك عن الملوك والاملاك فانت المعز بحسن الطاعة لأوليائك والمذل يخذلان المعاصي لقلوب اعدائك اسالك إن تعزني وتذل من ظلمني وتعاجل بخذلان كل شيطان وحاسد ومعاند وان تقويني بقوى لطفك يا معز يا مذل يا الله سبحانك إني كنت من الظالمين وصلى الله على سيدنا محمد وعلى آله واصحابه اجمعين.
descriptionEnglish: The Exalted says: “You honour whom You will and You humiliate whom You will.”⁽¹⁾ The Humiliator is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, afflicts humiliation upon who He wills from among His disobedient slaves.
• He is the One Who humiliates His enemies in accordance with justice due to their injustice, their contrariness and banishes them to the abode of punishment.

Humiliation is of various types and hues. Allah, Glory be To Him, the Exalted humiliates an oppressor with sickness, or in his possessions or in being in need of other than Allah. Oh Allah, take us from the humiliation of disobedience to the honour of obedience. Do not let any of the oppressors in Your creation humiliate us and crown us with the crown of Your honour. One of the Knowers of Allah said: “Allah will honour a slave to the extent to which he humiliates his self; Allah will humiliate a slave to the extent to which he returns to the delusions of the self.”

The name of Allah, the Humiliator, occurs a number of times in the Quran. For instance, in Sūrah ‘Arāf it says: “Certainly those who took the calf (for worship) wrath from their Lord and humiliation will come upon them in the life of this world.”⁽²⁾ And in Sūrah al-Mujādilah it says: “Those who oppose Allah and His Messenger will be the most humiliated.”⁽³⁾  And so forth.

The traveller to Allah, so that attains the stages of the soul’s perfection, must know the Truth by Himself and that Good comes because of working for Him. And thus when his focus becomes absorbed in witnessing the lights of Lordship he is cut off from thinking of anything other than Allah. He humiliates his self for the believing slaves of Allah and reins in his passions for the pleasure of Allah, the Truth, Glory be to Him, the Exalted- and this is unconditional honour. And the opposite of this is unconditional humiliation.

One of them has said: “Allah will honour a slave to the extent to which he humiliates his self; Allah will humiliate a slave to the extent to which he busies honouring the self.” Therefore, the traveller to Allah, if he humiliates his self, becomes honourable; if he honours his self he becomes humiliated.”

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever calls by this invocation- Allah will elevate his stature and he will obtain whatever he wants from the taming of the rebellious and oppressive.

Oh Allah, the Giver of Honour, Your Honour the honourable cannot resemble, and Your Self-Glory cannot be reached by the entire dominion- You are the Giver of Honour by regarding well the obedience of Your wards and You are the Humiliator by forsaking the disobedience in the hearts of Your enemies. I ask You to honour me and to humiliate those who oppress me and to forestall by forsaking every devil, envier and obstinate one and to strengthen me by the strength of Your gracious favours- oh Giver of Honour, oh Humiliator, oh Allah, Glory be to You. Indeed I am one of the oppressors. The Salutations and Peace of Allah be upon our Master Muhammad and upon his Family and all his Companions.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى في سورة المائدة «هو السميع العليم» فالسميع هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي لا يعزب عن ادراكه مسموع وان خفي،
• فهو السميع بغير جارحة
• وهو الذي يسمع نداء المضطرين ويجيب دعاء المحتاجين ويسمع حمد الحامدين،
• وهو الذي يسمع خطرات القلوب وهواجس النفوس ومناجاة الضمائر، يسمع كل نجوى ولا يخفى عليه شيء في الارض ولا في السماء.
وقد جاء في قول النبي عليه الصلاة والسلام «اللهم اني أعوذ بك من قول لا يسمع» أي دعاء لا يستجاب ولا يعتد به فكانه غير مسموع.

وقد يأتي السميع على اربعة معان: فالأول يراد به سمع الادراك ويتعلق بالأصوات كما في قوله تعالى «وقد سمع الله قول التي تجادلك في زوجها» والثاني سمع فهم وعقل ويتعلق بالمعاني مثل قوله تعالى «لا تقولوا راعنا وقولوا انظرنا واسمعوا» فليس المراد هنا سمع مجرد الكلام بل سمع الفهم والعقل، والمعنى الثالث يراد به سمع الاجابة، والمعنى الرابع يراد به سمع القبول والانقياد كما في قوله تعالى «سماعون للكذب» أي قابلون له ومنقادون له.

وقد ورد هذا الاسم الشريف أكثر من اربعين مرة في القرآن الكريم ففي سورة البقرة «فإنما هم في شقاق فسيكفيكهم الله وهو السميع العليم» وفي سورة الانعام «وله ما سكن في الليل والنهار وهو السميع العليم» وفي سورة الحج «وان الله سميع بصير» وغيرها.

وعلى السالك إلى الله حتى يفنى باسم الله السميع يجب عليه إن يحفظ لسانه من الباطل إلا بخير وان يكون في كل نفس من انفاسه في مراقبة الحق سبحانه وتعالى حتى يتحقق بقول الحبيب عليه الصلاة والسلام «الاحسان إن تعبد الله كأنك تراه فان لم تكن تراه فانه يراك» وان يتقرب إلى مولاه بالنوافل حتى يفيض الحق على قلبه نورا ويهبه مددا وخيرا بأنوار هذا الاسم الشريف.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم انه من قرأه خمسمائة مرة بعد صلاة الضحى ودعا إلى الله وذكر حاجته استجاب الله دعاءه وقضى حاجته ببركة وسر هذا الاسم.
ومن علم بأن الله سميع حفظ لسانه فلا يتكلم إلا بالخير، ومن أكثر ذكره بلا عدد بعد تأدية الفريضة، لم ترد له دعوة، وكان في قومه مسموع القول، مطاع الكلمة.

فيا الله انت السميع لحركات القلوب وخواطر النفوس، السميع لنداء المضطرين مجيب دعاء المحتاجين اشرق يا ربي على سمعي نورا منك به اسمع تسبيح الكائنات في الارض والسماوات وامنحني قوة روحية اسمع بها عهد «الست بربكم» انك على كل شيء قدير،
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says in Sūrah al-Mā’idah: “He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.”⁽¹⁾ The All-Hearing is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, is the One Who comprehends everything heard, even if it concealed.
• He is the All-Hearing without using any organ.
• He is the One Who listens to the call of the destitute, the supplication of the needy, and the praise of the ones who praise.

• He is the One Who hears the thoughts of the heart, the ideas of the self, and the confidences of the conscience. He hears every secret conversation and nothing in the heavens and the earth is concealed from Him.

The Prophet, peace be upon him is reported to have said: “Oh Allah, I seek refuge in You from a statement that is not heard.”⁽²⁾ That is, from a supplication that is not accepted, one that is not considered as if it was not heard.

Hearing can be seen to have four meanings. In the first meaning He hears and comprehends that which is connected to sounds such as in the statement:  “Indeed Allah has heard the statement of her that disputes with you concerning her husband.”⁽³⁾ In the second He hears the understanding and purport of statements and this is connected to meanings, such as in the Exalted’s statement: “Oh you who believe! Say not “Rā‘inah” [Listen to us and we will listen to you] but say make us understand and hear.”⁽⁴⁾ The intent here is not hearing the word in isolation, but in hearing its purport. By the third is intended the hearing of response and by the fourth is intended the hearing of acceptance and following, such as in the Exalted’s statement: “They like to listen to falsehood.”⁽⁵⁾ That is, they accept and follow it.

This name occurs more than 40 times in the Noble Quran. In Sūrah Baqarah it is stated: “Then they are in opposition. So Allah will suffice for you against them. And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.”⁽⁶⁾ Sūrah al-An‘ām says: “And to Him belongs whatever exists in the night and day. And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.”⁽⁷⁾ Sūrah Ḥajj states: “And indeed Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.”⁽⁸⁾

The traveller to Allah, in order to be absorbed into the name of Allah, the All-Hearing, must preserve his tongue from falsehood and occupy it with goodness. He must be aware of Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, at every breath until he realises the saying of the Beloved, upon whom be Salutations and Peace: “Righteousness (Iḥsān) is that You worship Allah as if You see Him, and if You cannot see Him, then indeed [be aware that] He sees You.”⁽⁹⁾ He must approach His Protector with additional acts of worship until Allah, the Truth, lets his heart overflow with light and bestows upon him health and goodness by the lights of this Noble name.

Among the special qualities of this name is that whoever recites it 500 times after the ṣalāh of Ḍuḥā and supplicates to Allah mentioning his need- Allah will accept his supplication and fulfil his need by the blessings of this name.

Once you know that Allah is All-Hearing, you will not talk except good things. Whoever repeat that name endlessly, after doing the obligatory devotion, Allah will not turn down any invocation he makes; and people will listen to him and obey.

Oh Allah, You are the Hearer of the movements of the heart and the ideas of the self, the Hearer of the call of the destitute, and the One Who answers the supplication of the needy- let our hearing, my Lord, radiate with light from You. Through it  I will hear Your glorification by the existents in the earth and in the Heavens. Grant me the spiritual faculty by which I hear the covenant: “Am I not Your Lord?” Indeed You have power over all things.
refEnglish: (2)Aḥmad, relating Anas bin Mālik, Allah be pleased with him.
(9)Muslim relating ‘Umar bin al-Khaṭṭāb, Allah be pleased with him.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى: «إنه هو السميع البصير»
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي يشاهد الاشياء كلها ظاهرها وخفيها
• فهو بصير خائنة الأعين وما تخفي الصدور يشاهد
• ويرى ولا يغيب عنه ما في السماوات العلى وما في الارض وما بينهما وما تحت الثرى.

وقد قال الإمام القشيري في وصف اسمي الله السميع والبصير انهما صفتان لله سبحانه وتعالى فلا يخرج مسموع عن سمعه، ولا موجود عن بصره ولا يحجبهما شيء فيسمع السر والنجوى ويبصر ما تحت الثرى. ومن عرف إن الله سميع بصير كان من أدبه دوام المراقبة ومطالبة النفس بدقيق المحاسبة، وحفظ سمعه وبصره لله تعالى. عندئذ أحبه الله وكان له سمعا وبصرا فيه يسمع وبه يبصر كما جاء في الحديث الشريف «فاذا احببته: كنت سمعه الذي يسمع به، وبصره الذي يبصر به».

وقد ورد اسم الله البصير في اكثر من اربعين موضعا، ففي سورة البقرة «إن الله بما تعملون بصير» وفي سورة آل عمران «والله بصير بالعباد» وفي سورة غافر «والله يقضي بالحق والذين يدعون من دونه لا يقضون بشيء إن الله هو السميع البصير» وغيرها.

والسالك إلى الله اذا اراد إن يشرق الحق على قلبه نور هذا الاسم الشريف عليه إن يعلم إن الله خلق له البصر والبصيرة لينظر إلى الآيات وعجائب الملكوت والسماوات فلا يكون نظره إلا عبرة، وان يعلم إن الله يراه ويسمعه في كل الاحوال ولا يستهن بنظر الله اليه واطلاعه عليه، ومن اخفى عن غير الله ما لا يخفيه عن الله فقد استهان بنظر الله تعالى، والمراقبة هي من احدى ثمرات الإحسان ونجاح سير وسلوك المريد إلى الله تعالى كما قال عليه الصلاة والسلام: «الاحسان إن تعبد الله كأنك تراه فان لم تكن تراه فانه يراك» لذلك احفظ اعضاءك وجوارحك يا سالك عن إن تقرب أي معصية حتى تحظى وتفوز برضا مولاك عليك وكما قال سبحانه وتعالى في سورة الاسراء «وكفى بربك بذنوب عباده خبيرا بصيرا».

ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من لازم على ذكره بعد صلاة الجمعة مائة مرة بأن يقول: (يا الله يا بصير) -دون أن يتكلم مع أحد -طهر الله سريرته، وأنار بصيرته، ووفقه لصالح القول والعمل بإذنه تعالى، ومن كان لله كان الله له.
ومن ذكر الاسم بعد صلاة الجمعة مائة مرة نور الله له بصيرته .

فيا رباه انت البصير بعيوبي، الخبير بذنوبي، المطلع على سري، بيدك زمام امري، اسالك إن تجعل في قلبي نورا وفي بصري نورا لأشاهد حقائق الاشياء، وأتأدب معك بالظاهر والخفاء واجعلنا يا الله لك مشاهدين وفي حماك قائمين وعلى اعتابك واقفين إنك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “Indeed He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.”⁽¹⁾
• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, is the One Who witnesses all things- both its outward and its concealed aspects.

• He sees the furtive glances of the eyes and what hearts conceal. He sees and witnesses and nothing that is in the highest heavens and in the earth, what is between them and what is beneath the soil is hidden from Him.

Imām Qushairī, in his description of the two names of Allah, the All-Hearing and All-Seeing, says that they are two attributes of Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, so that nothing heard escapes His hearing and nothing that exists escapes His seeing. Nothing is veiled from Him and He hears the secret conversations and sees what is beneath the soil. Whoever knows that Allah is the All-Hearing and All-Seeing is one who is in constant awareness of Allah and subjects his self to the most intense accounting. He preserves his sight and hearing for the sake of Allah, the Exalted until when Allah loves him, He becomes such a one’s hearing and sight- by Him he hears and by Him he sees. As the ḥadīth qudsī says: “When I love him I become his hearing by which he hears and his sight by which he sees.”⁽²⁾

The name  of Allah, the Seeing, occurs more than forty times in the Quran. In Sūrah al-Baqarah it says: “Indeed Allah sees what you do.”⁽³⁾  In Sūrah Āl ‘Imrān it says: “And Allah is All-Seer of His slaves.”⁽⁴⁾   And in Sūrah Ghāfir: “Allah judges with truth. Those whom they call upon besides Allah cannot judge anything. Indeed Allah is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.” And so forth.

The traveller to Allah, if he desires that his heart be radiated by Allah with the light of this noble name, must know that Allah has given him sight to see the signs and wonders of the heavenly dominions and thus his gift of sight is but a warning to him. He must know that Allah sees and hears him in every state and he must not make light of Allah’s watching and awareness of him. Whoever hides from that which is other than Allah cannot hide from Allah and such a one has made light of Allah’s watching him. The constant awareness of Allah’s watching is one of the fruits of Divine watchfulness [Ihsan] and of a murīd’s successful journey to Allah, the Exalted. As the Prophet, upon whom be Salutations and Peace, said: “Divine watchfulness [Iḥsān] is that you worship Allah as if you see Him, and if you cannot see Him, then indeed [be aware that] He sees you.”⁽⁵⁾ Therefore, oh traveller, guard your organs and limbs from approaching disobedience so that you succeed in enjoying the favour of your Protector’s pleasure. As Allah, the Exalted, says in Sūrah Isrā’: “And sufficient is your Lord as the All-Aware of and All-Seeing of the sins of His slaves.”⁽⁶⁾

Among the special qualities of this noble name is that whoever recites it constantly a hundred times after the ṣalāh of Jumu‘ah will have his sight enlightened by Allah and He will grant him righteous words and deeds, by the permission of Allah.
Whoever mentions this name a hundred times after the Jumu’a prayer, Allah will enlighten his insight.

Oh Lord, You are the One Who sees my faults, Who is aware of my sins, well-acquainted with my secret being. In Your Hand are the reins of my affairs. I ask You that You place in my heart a light and in my vision a light so that I see the realities of things and so that I behave properly with You outwardly and in secret. Make us, oh Allah, witnesses for You, standing under Your protection, halting at Your threshold. Indeed You have power over all things.
refEnglish: (2)Bukhari, relating Abū Hurayrah, Allah be pleased with him.
(5)Muslim relating ‘Umar bin al-Khaṭṭāb, Allah be pleased with him.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «إن الحكم إلا لله عليه توكلت وعليه فليتوكل المتوكلون» فالحكم هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• فهو صاحب الفصل بين الحق والباطل
• والمميز بين الشقي والسعيد بالعقاب والثواب،
• وهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي حكم على القلوب بالرضا والقناعة وعلى النفوس بالانقياد والطاعة،
• وهو الحاكم النافذ حكمه الذي لا راد لقضائه ولا معقب لحكمه
• المظهر الحق من الباطل
• والمنصف للمظلوم من الظالم،
وان اسم الله الحكيم هي من صفات الذات له تبارك وتعالى.
وقد ورد اسم الله الحكم في عدة مواضع في القران الكريم نذكر منها في سورة الانعام «أفغير الله أبتغي حكما وهو الذي انزل اليكم الكتاب مفصلا» وفي سورة التين «اليس الله بأحكم الحاكمين» وغيرها.

وقد قال بعض العارفين إن الحق تبارك وتعالى قد كان حكمه بجميع الكليات والجزئيات قد حصل من الازل إلى الابد، لذلك على العبد إن لا يطلب إلا ما يسره الله له ولا يحزن على ما تعسر عليه لان اعمال العباد مرهونة بيد الحكم سبحانه وتعالى.

فعلى السالك إلى الله حتى يفنى باسم الله الحكم عليه إن يسلم لأقدار الله تبارك وتعالى ويعلم انه سبحانه وتعالى إن يحكم فهو بمقتضى العدل فهو اعلم بمن يستحق السعادة ومن يستحق الشقاوة وان كل انسان مسير لما خلق له وعليه إن لا يشغل قلبه إلا بما يقربه ويوصله من الله سبحانه وتعالى فمن كانت بدايته مشرقة كانت نهايته مشرقة عندها سيشرق الحكم على قلبه انوار هذا الاسم الشريف.
ولهذا الاسم خواص كثيرة نذكر منها انه من أكثر من ذكر هذا الاسم الشريف بعد كل صلاة رزقه الله الفهم والحكمة وأصبح من اهل القرب والوصال بإذنه تعالى.
ومن ذكر هذا الاسم في جوف الليل على طهارة تامة جعل الله باطنه موطن الاسرار الربانية، وظاهره مشرق الأنوار الرحمانية.

فيا الله انت الحكم الذي حكمت على العوالم وهي في علمك وخصصتها بإرادتك وابرزتها بقدرتك فكل شيء في الوجود محكوم عليه فيما هو من علم وعمل وقد نشرت اسباب السعادة لأهلها، فاشهد يا رباه قلوبنا اقدارك حتى نتنعم في انوارك ونرضى عن احكامك، فنصبح بالرضا من خواص خدمك إنك على كل شيء قدير.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “The decision rests only with Allah. In Him do I put my trust and let all those who trust put their trust in Him.”⁽¹⁾The Arbiter is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One Who makes distinction between truth and falsehood and
• He is the One Who distinguishes the eternally unfortunate from the eternally fortunate, by way of punishment and reward.
• He, the Exalted, is the One Who imposes upon the hearts pleasure and contentedness with Him, and the One Who rules upon the self to follow and obey Him.
• He is the Governor Who decisions are executed – One Whose decree cannot be repelled nor His order revised.
• He is the One Who manifests truth from falsehood and sees that the right of the oppressed is established in face of the oppressor.
The name of Allah, the Arbiter, is one of the Names that belong to Allah’s Essence, Blessed and Exalted is He.

The name of Allah, the Arbiter, occurs in a number of places in the Quran. For instance, in Sūrah An‘ām it is stated: “Say: Shall I seek a judge other than Allah while it is He Who has sent unto you the Book, explained in detail.”⁽²⁾In Sūrah Tīn it is stated: “Is Allah not the Best of Judges.”⁽³⁾

One of the Knowers of Allah said that Allah’s rule governs all things, both the universal and particular- and this was from prior to the beginning of creation to eternity. Therefore a slave must only request that which Allah has facilitated for him and must not become sad at the difficulties he encounters since all the acts of the slaves are subject to the governance of Allah, the Exalted.

The traveller to Allah, so that he becomes absorbed in the name of Allah, the Arbiter, must content himself with the decree of Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He and he must know that when Allah governs, it is in accordance with justice and that He best knows who deserves everlasting bliss and who deserves everlasting punishment. He must know that every human being is facilitated for what he has been created and he must not occupy his heart except with that which brings him closer to, and lets him arrive at, Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted. Whoever’s beginning is radiant, such a one’s end is radiant when the Arbiter will let his heart radiate with the light of this noble name.

This name has many special qualities. Among them is that whoever frequently recites this noble name after every ṣalāh Allah will grant such a one understanding and wisdom and make him of the people of nearness and arrival, by the permission of Allah.
Whoever repeats this name in the mid of the night, with full ablution, Allah makes his heart a place of Divine secrets, and makes his face gracefully illuminating.

Oh Allah! You are the Arbiter Who governs over all the worlds. They were in Your knowledge and You particularized them through Your will and brought them into existence through Your decree. Everything in existence is governed by You, whether it is in knowledge or deed. You have diffused the causes of endless bliss for those given it. Let our hearts, oh Lord, witness Your decrees so that we are blessed by Your illuminations and so that we are pleased by Your governance. Thus we will be content exclusively with Your service. Indeed You have power over all things.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «وتمت كلمت ربك صدقا وعدلا لا مبدل لكلماته»
• فهو الذي لا يظلم ولا يجور
• وهو المنزه عن الظلم والجور في احكامه وأفعاله،
• فهو الذي يعطي كل ذي حق حقه، ويضع كل شيء موضعه ولا يصدر منه إلا العدل.

وكما يقول الإمام الغزالي عن وصف اسم الله العدل انه لن يعرف العادل من لم يعرف عدله ولا يعرف عدله من لا يعرف فضله، فمن اراد إن يفهم هذا الوصف ينبغي عليه إن يحيط علما بأفعال الله تعالى من اعلى ملكوت السماوات إلى منتهى الثرى كما ورد إن بالعدل قامت السماوات والارض.

وعلى السالك إلى الله حتى يتخلق بأنوار هذا الاسم الشريف يجب عليه إن يكون عدلا في احكامه واوصافه وأفعاله فلا يظلم احدا ويعدل بين روحه ونفسه وبين جوارحه وجسمه فيعطي للروح رتبة الشرف والاحترام ويعطي للنفس رتبة الادب والوقوف عند الحدود، أما وكذلك يجب عليه ألا يعترض على احكام وافعال وتدابير مولاه سواء اوافق مراده ام لم يوافق مراده لان مرادك يا سالك هو رضا مولاك عليك.
وقد ورد ذكر اسم العدل في مواضع منها «وإذا حكمتم بين الناس إن تحكموا بالعدل» وقوله «إن الله يأمر بالعدل والاحسان» وغيرها.
ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من دوام على ذكره حصل له انتشار العلم وسخر الله له الناس والقلوب ليكونوا اعوانا له على الطاعة والقرب.
ومن لازم ذكره اثنتين وتسعين مرة قبل طلوع الشمس وكان حاكما ألهمه الله العدل في رعيته، ووفقه لما فيه الخير لأمته.

فتجلى يا رباه لي بنور اسمك العدل حتى اعدل بين روحي ونفسي وجسمي فأنال سر الخلافة عنك واتلقى مدد المعونة منك لأحظى برضاك مع الاخيار والابرار في جنات تجري من تحتها الانهار، فانت يا الله رفعت قوما وخفضت اخرين بالعدل قدرت في الازل فريقا وفي الجنة وفريقا في السعير إنك على كل شيء قدير فانت نعم المولى ونعم النصير.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “And the Word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and in justice. None can change His Words.”⁽¹⁾
• He is the One Who does not oppress and or do wrong.
• He is far above oppressing or wrong-doing in His rulings and acts.
• He is the One Who gives everyone his due right and the One Who puts everything into its proper place. Nothing emanates from Him except justice.

As Imām Ghazālī has said in describing the name of Allah, the Just, that no-one can ever be recognized as a just person whose justice is not recognized; and no-one’s justice can be recognized unless his graces are recognized. Thus it is necessary for whoever wishes to understand this description that he comprehends, through knowledge, the acts of Allah, the Exalted- whether pertaining to the highest of heavens or to the end of the soil. And as has been transmitted: the heavens and the earth are established through justice.

The traveller to Allah, so that he is moulded by the lights of this noble name, must be just in his rulings, person and acts. He must not be unjust towards anybody and he treats equitably his soul and self, his limbs and body. Thus he must give his soul the requisite degree of honour and respect and imbue his self with the necessary discipline and stop it from crossing the boundaries of the Sharī‘ah. He must accord the heart the grade of preserving him so that he continuously and everlastingly is in a state of presence with Allah, Glory be to Him, the Exalted. Similarly, he must not oppose the rulings, acts and administration of His Protector, whether it is in agreement with his purpose or not- since your purpose, oh traveller, must be your Protector’s pleasure with you.
This name occurs in a number of places in the Quran: “And that when you judge between men you judge with justice.”⁽²⁾ “Indeed Allah commands to justice and righteousness.”⁽³⁾ And so forth.

Among the special qualities of this name is that whoever is continuous in its recitation knowledge will be unfolded for him and Allah will make people and hearts of service and assistance to him in being obedient and coming closer to Allah.
If a ruling person repeats that name 92 times before the sun rise, Allah will inspire him justice among people and guide him for the general welfare.

Oh Lord, present Yourself to me with the light of Your name, the Just, so that I equitably treat my soul, self and body. Thus I obtain the core of vicegerency from You, support and assistance from You and attain Your pleasure with the chosen and the righteous in gardens beneath which rivers flow. Oh Allah, You elevate some people and abase others through justice. You have decreed in eternity a group in Paradise and a group in Hell. Indeed You have power over all things. You are the best of Protectors, the best of Helpers.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «الله لطيف بعباده» واللطيف هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى.
• فهو الذي يلطف بعباده من حيث لا يعلمون ويهيئ مصالحهم من حيث لا يحتسبون.
• وهو العالم بخفايا الامور ودقائق الاشياء،
• وهو الذي يريد بعباده الخير واليسر ويفيض لهم اسباب الصلاح والبر.
وقد ورد ذكر اسم الله اللطيف في القرآن سبع مرات ففي سورة الانعام «وهو اللطيف الخبير» وفي سورة يوسف «إن ربي لطيف لما يشاء انه هو العليم الحكيم» وفي سورة الاحزاب «إن الله كان لطيفا خبيرا».

وقد اقترن ذكر اسم الله اللطيف باسم الله الخبير ففي قوله تعالى «وهو اللطيف الخبير» فاللطيف باستخراج الاشياء الخبير بمكانها، اللطيف بذاته، الباطن في غيب وجوده والخبير بدقائق الاشياء ولطائفها بحيث لا يغرب عن إدراكه ألطف أرواحها وأقواها ولا أدق جواهرها وأعراضها.

ومن علامة فناء السالك إلى الله بهذا الاسم الشريف هو إن ترى بينه وبين الله سر عظيم لا يعرفه إلا سر اللطيف. يسري هذا السر في الفؤاد في السر وفي سر السر. ولا يشهد صاحب هذا السريان إلا صاحب القلب الكبير العارف بالله المربي الكامل لان تجليات اسم الله الطيف لا تحصى فهي تجليات ظاهرة وباطنة.

ومن خواص هذا الاسم الشريف انه من أكثر من ذكره وسع الله عليه وكان ملطوفا به في كل اموره، ودفع الله عنه كل الالام وقضى الله حاجاته بإذنه تعالى وبسر اسم الله اللطيف، وقال بعض العارفين من قرأ «الله لطيف بعباده يرزق من يشاء وهو القوي العزيز» في كل يوم تسع مرات لطف الله به في اموره ورزقه رزقا واسعا.

فيا الله يا لطيف إن الطافك احاطت بالموجودات وعمت بالكائنات وان لك نفحات اذا سرت في قلب غافل ايقظته وان لك لحظات جعلت اولياءك عندك في اعلى الدرجات فيا لطيف الالطاف الطف بنا عند سؤال الملائكة الاطهار واشهدنا تجلي اللطف في النفس والافاق فانت الواحد الاحد الخلاق.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “Allah is kind towards His slaves.”⁽¹⁾ The Kind is one of the Grand Names of Allah.
• He is the One Who takes care of for His slaves in ways they are not aware of; and paves the way for their bests interests in ways they cannot reckon.
• He is the Knower of the hidden and most subtle aspects of things.
• He is the One Who facilitates goodness and ease for His slaves and abundantly pours forth for them the ways to righteousness and goodness.

The name of Allah, the Kind, occurs seven times in the Quran. For instance, Sūrah An‘ām states: “He is the Kind, the Aware.”⁽²⁾ Sūrah Yūsuf states: “Indeed my Lord is Most Kind unto whom He wills.”⁽³⁾ Sūrah Aḥzāb states: “Indeed Allah is Ever Most Kind, Well-Aware with what you do.”⁽⁴⁾

The name of Allah, the Very Kind, is mentioned together with Allah’s name, the Aware. Thus, for instance, the Exalted says: “He is the Kind, the Aware.”⁽⁵⁾ He is Most Kind with regard to the making and extraction of things and Most Aware with regard to their position. He is Most Kind by His very Essence- the Inner by the unseen-ness of His existence. He is aware of the subtlest, most delicate aspects of things in that nothing escapes His comprehension- neither the subtlest spirit or subtlest faculty, nor the most delicate of substances and qualities.

Among the signs that the traveller to Allah is absorbed in this noble name is that you see a great innermost relationship established between him and Allah. This relationship is not exposed except to those with similar innermost. The soul of this innermost relationship flows into the heart, into the core and then into the innermost. This flow is seen only by one with great heart, the Knower of Allah, and Perfect Coach. The presentations of this name, the Kind, are countless; they are manifested outwardly and inwardly.

Among the special qualities of this name is that whoever abundantly recites it will be shown generosity by Allah and He will be most kind with him in every affair. Allah will protect him from every pain and grant him his needs by His permission through the mystery of His name, the Most Kind.

One of the Knowers of Allah said: “Whoever recites the verse “Allah is Most Kind with His slaves. He gives provisions to whom He wills. And He is the All-Strong, the Almighty”⁽⁶⁾ nine times every day- such a one will be dealt treated with immense kindness by Allah in his affairs and He will give him a wide provision.

Oh Allah, oh Most Kind- Your kindness encompasses all existents, embracing every being. Indeed You have fragrant breezes that if they penetrate the heart of the forgetful one will revive him. With You are the  glances that give Your saints elevated degrees with You. Oh Immensely Kind, show kindness to us when the pure angels question us and let us witness the illuminations of kindness in the self and in the horizons. You are the One, the Only and the Creator of All.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «لا تدركه الابصار وهو يدرك الابصار وهو اللطيف الخبير» فالخبير هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• فهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي لا تعزب عنه الاخبار الباطنة.
• ولا يجري في الملك والملكوت شيء ولا تتحرك ذرة ولا تسكن ولا تضطرب نفس ولا تطمئن إلا ويكون عنده علم بذلك.

وقد ورد اسم الله الخبير في القران المجيد اكثر من اربعين مرة ففي سورة آل عمران «لكيلا تحزنوا على ما فاتكم ولا ما اصابكم والله خبير بما تعملون» وفي سورة الحج «الم تر إن الله انزل من السماء ماء فتصبح الارض مخضرة إن الله لطيف خبير» وفي سورة النمل «انه خبير بما تفعلون» وغيرها.

وإذا اراد السالك إلى الله إن يفنى باسم الله الخبير فيجب عليه إن يكون خبيرا بدسائس نفسه، بصيرا بخدائع حسه يعرف الفرق بين خطرات الملك والشيطان، بصيرا بالهامات الرحمن، خبيرا بأحواله محترزا في اقواله وافعاله، وإذا علم السالك الى الله ان مولاه مطلع على سره وقلبه عليم بخفاياه يكتفي برفع همته الى الله واستحضار حاجته في قلبه من غير ان ينطق بلسانه عندئذ جدير بهذا السالك الى الله ان يسمى بين العباد خبيرا.

ولهذا الاسم الشريف خواص كثيرا نذكر منها انه من اراد ان يرى شيئا في منامه يريد معرفته فليقرأ عند نومه «الا يعلم من خلق وهو اللطيف الخبير»  فسيرى ما يكشف له وجه الصواب فيها، إن شاء الله تعالى. وقد قال الامام زين العابدين: من اراد عزا بلا عشيرة، وهيبة بلا سلطان، وغنى بلا فقر فليخرج من ذل المعصية الى عز الطاعة.

إلهي انت الخبير بالدقائق والبصائر المطلع على السرائر، الناظر الى الضمائر، تجلى لي يا الله بنور اسمك الخبير بلا حول مني ولا تدبير حتى اكون خبيرا بالأمور الغائبة عن الجهال وانجو من الشرك الخفي وما هو اخفى في الاقوال والاعمال ويتجلى لي مولاي الخبير فانت نعم المولى ونعم النصير.
descriptionEnglish: Allah, the Exalted, says: “No vision can grasp Him, but He grasps all vision. He is the Most Subtle, the Fully Aware.”⁽¹⁾ The Fully aware is one of the most Grand Names of Allah.
• He, Glory be to Him, the Exalted, is the One from whom no insider news can escape;
• Nothing may escape his knowledge in any domain, seen or unseen; no atom moves or stand still but He knows about; no soul may be disturbed or calmed except that He knows about.

The name of Allah, the Fully Aware, occurs more than forty times in the Glorious Quran. Thus in Sūrah Āl ‘Imrān it states: “To teach you not to grieve for that which escaped you, nor for that which had befallen you. And Allah is Fully Aware of all that you do.”⁽²⁾ Sūrah Ḥajj states: “See you not that Allah sends down water from the sky and then the earth becomes green? Indeed Allah is the Most Kind, the Fully Aware.”⁽³⁾ Sūrah Naml states: “Indeed He is Fully Aware of what you do.”⁽⁴⁾ And so forth.

If the traveller to Allah wishes to become absorbed in Allah’s name, the Aware, he must himself be aware of the schemes of his self, understand the deceptions of his sentiments, and be cognizant of the difference between angelic and satanic inclinations. He should discern the inspirations of the Merciful, aware of his states and guard his speech and acts. When the traveller to Allah knows [for certain] that Allah is acquainted with his hidden-most self, his heart and his secrets then it is suffices for him to determine himself towards Allah and to evoke his need in his heart without expressing it through his tongue. At this point the traveller to Allah becomes worthy of being named as one who is aware (khabīr).

This name has many special qualities. Among them is that whoever desires to know about something in his sleep can recite “Does not He know what He has created! And He is the Most Subtle, and Aware.”⁽⁵⁾ Imām Zayn ul ‘Ābidīn “Whoever desires honour without kinsfolk, awe without authority and richness beyond need, let him proceed from the debasement of disobedience to the dignity of obedience.”

Oh my God, You are the Fully Aware of all subtleties and finer things, the Seer and One notified of all secrets and mysteries, the One who looks at hearts- illuminate me, oh Allah, with the light of Your name the Aware, without any change or step from me, so that I become aware of the hidden matters as distinct from the ignorant and that I be saved from hidden polytheism and what is more hidden in speech and act. My Protector, the Aware, will illuminate me- for You are the best of Protectors, the Best of Helpers.

descriptionArabic: قال تعالى «واعلموا ان الله غفور حليم» فالحليم هو اسم من اسماء الله الحسنى،
• والحلم هو الاناة والعقل وحلم الله هو تأخيره العقوبة عن المستحق لها وهو الذي يشاهد معصية العصاة ويرى مخالفة الاوامر ولا يسارع في الانتقام مع غاية الاقتدار،
• والحليم هو الذي غفر بعد ما ستر وهو سبحانه وتعالى الذي يحفظ الود ويحسن العهد وينجز الوعد.
ولا شك ان سيد الحلماء هو سيدنا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فانه لم يغضب لنفسه ابدا فقد الحق به الكفار كافة انواع العذاب ومع ذلك كان حليما يقول: اللهم اغفر لقومي فانهم لا يعلمون.
وفي الحديث الشريف عن ابي موسى عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال «ما احد اصبر على أذى  يسمعه من الله تعالى، انهم يجعلون له ندا ويجعلون له ولدا وهو مع ذلك يرزقهم ويعافيهم ويعطيهم».

وقد ورد ذكر اسم الله الحليم في القرآن الكريم اكثر من عشر مرات نذكر منها في سورة البقرة «والله غني حليم» وفي سورة الاحزاب «والله يعلم ما في قلوبكم وكان الله عليما حليما» وفي سورة التغابن «والله شكور حليم» وغيرها...
